programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo hits the West End to explore an exotic store, which was a favourite among Edwardian ladies. At Covent Gardens Royal Ballet School he hears how in 1909 a Russian ballet company took London by storm and how its prima ballerina inspired the schools founding choreographer. Leaving the capital from Charing Cross, Michael heads for Dartford in Kent, where he discovers the origins of netball and gymslips at the North Kent College.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Warwick to Rye in East Sussex. In the orchards of East Malling, Kent, Michael discovers that the Edwardians serious attitude towards cultivation bore fruit. Rootstock developed at the NIAB Centre for Fruit Research, established in 1913, is today responsible for much of world apple production.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Beginning just outside Newcastle in Jarrow, Michael uncovers the desperation which led 200 men to march 300 miles to Westminster in order to petition the Government for work. In Newcastle, Michael admires the citys iconic railway bridge before heading to Byker, where he discovers a new innovation in greyhound racing.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Steered by his 1936 Bradshaws Guide, Michael Portillo is in Northumbria en route to the Highlands. On this leg, he explores Kielder Forest, beginning at the County Show in Stocksfield. Michael discovers what lay behind a national initiative to plant one of the largest man-made woodlands in Europe. Crossing the border to Scotland, Michael arrives in the weaving town of Hawick to visit Lovat Mill, where, in the 1930s, tweed was big business.

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: British troops rescue Edith and Rene and the Germans decide to escape to Spain.

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: The Colonel and Gruber make an escape bid to Spain, Herr Flick has had extensive plastic surgery, and Rene dithers over whether to continue his affair with Yvette or go back to his loving wife.

programme.title: Allo Allo!
programme.desc: Communists capture Rene and Flick and Von Smallhausen try to fool Edith.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Life on-board a narrowboat with Robbie Cumming. Robbie tackles a propeller problem and tricky locks on the Trent and Mersey Canal in Derbyshire.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: A low canal tunnel in Staffordshire proves a problem and Robbie helps out a stranded boater.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: A fallen tree and a leaking lock pound hamper Robbies journey across the Pennines.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Robbie runs aground on the Rochdale Canal, will he make it to Manchester?

programme.title: Dom Chineas Cornish Workshop
programme.desc: Dom builds a drift car with engineer Sam Lovegrove. At home, he gets a digger in to make his new pond, and he and Sam have a go at gig rowing after fixing the club trailer in Hayle.

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeve continues his travels along the Tropic of Capricorn, the line of latitude that cuts across South Africa, South America and Australia. On the Australian west coast, Simon witnesses the migration of the humpback whale. He meets a tough outback couple refusing to leave an asbestos polluted ghost town and a farmer attempting to eke a living out of a dustbowl.

programme.title: Tropic of Capricorn
programme.desc: Simon Reeves journey around the world along the Tropic of Capricorn is the most spectacular stage yet. Beginning in Chile, Simon crosses the Atacama desert and climbs 15,000 feet into the Andes. In Argentina he dodges angry South American bees as he hunts for honey with the indigenous Wichi people, and in Brazil he tastes the delights of Sao Paolo, the biggest city in the Southern Hemisphere.

programme.title: David Jasons Great British Inventions
programme.desc: Sir David Jason finds out about the Penny Farthing as he looks at the British inventors who played a vital part in the evolution of the wheel.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Derek navigates the dry stone walls of the Peak District to collect a very original 1988 Opel Manta, Dave handles a rare Ford Granada rally support vehicle, and Paul contends with a Hilux monster truck.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Daves on his way to Grimsby to pick up an iconic 80s fast Ford, the Mk1 Fiesta XR2.

programme.desc: Shopping from home.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillos railway journey through 1930s Britain from Newcastle to Loch Ness reaches Falkirk in Scotland. Here he discovers the Westerglen Transmitting Station, from where they continue to broadcast analogue radio signal to the Scottish borders. Following the route of the old Caledonian Railway Company Michael discovers the `Riviera of the Highlands.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Stopping at Dundee, Michael heads for Glamis Castle, where Lady Elizabeth Bowes-Lyon, the Queen Mother grew up. Michael hears about her happy childhood and how she later found sanctuary there, when King Edward VIII abdicated and she and Prince Albert unexpectedly became King and Queen of the United Kingdom. Striking north along the east coast of Scotland Michaels next stop is Montrose from where he makes an excursion into the Eastern Highlands.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in the Scottish Highlands on the last leg of his rail journey through 1930s Britain. He begins in Elgins port, the coastal town of Lossiemouth, where James Ramsay MacDonald, the Labour Partys first Prime Minister was born. Michael meets his granddaughter to visit the family home and discovers what shaped his politics. On the outskirts of Lossiemouth, Michael finds a remote boarding school established in the 1930s.

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo boards the Great Western Railway at the Cornish seaside resort of St Ives. In Britains first studio pottery, he attempts a decorative wax technique and feels the heat of the firing kiln. He discovers a Cornish fisherman, who,although he began painting only in his seventies, inspired established artists from the capital.

programme.title: Bangers and Cash
programme.desc: Jack has his eye on a Peugeot 205 GTI that could be one of the finest examples in the country. Plus, Derek collects a pair of sporty 60s Triumphs.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: On the last leg of his journey across England, Robbie crosses an epic aqueduct near Stratford-upon-Avon and gets stuck in a lock in central Birmingham.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater Robbie Cumming begins his 170-mile journey across the north of England and soon runs into difficulty when he encounters a propeller problem in Wigan and runs aground in Blackburn. Nevertheless, he pushes on to navigate a true wonder of the waterways, the Burnley Embankment.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Narrowboater and film-maker Robbie Cumming crosses the Pennines on the picturesque Leeds and Liverpool Canal, and lends a hand to hire boaters in Gargrave, North Yorkshire. He also encounters Bingley Five Rise Locks, a wonder of the inland waterways.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries
programme.desc: Jump on board with narrowboater Robbie Cumming as he explores the Victorian village of Saltaire on the Leeds and Liverpool Canal, moors up in Leeds and goes in search of an abandoned waterway on the Aire and Calder Navigation.

programme.title: Canal Boat Diaries

programme.title: Iqbal
programme.desc: Iqbal is a young man born deaf and dumb into a Muslim family in rural India. Just like many of Indias young hopefuls obsessed with the national sport, Iqbals secret dream is to play cricket for his country. However, his father is set against it. Through the help of his resourceful, feisty sister, Iqbal at last finds a coach and mentor: alcoholic ex-cricket star Mohit.
